files.content:
---
title: Privacy Policy
description: Our commitment to protecting your privacy
---

import { siteConfig } from "@/config/site-config";

# Privacy Policy

Last Updated: 2025-07-15

## 1. Introduction

This Privacy Policy explains how we collect, use, and protect your personal information when you use our Next.js starter kit, related services, and website (collectively, the "Services"). By using {siteConfig.name}, you consent to the collection and use of your information in accordance with this Privacy Policy. All use of your personal information is aligned with our Terms and Conditions, which are available for your review anytime.

## 2. Information We Collect

When you sign up, show interest in our offerings, or get in touch, we collect some personal details you provide. Here’s what we might ask for to make your experience smoother and more personalized:

- Email Address
- Names or identifiers
- Profile information
- Job Titles
- Usernames
- Passwords
- Contact Preferences
- Authentication Data
- Billing Addresses
- Any additional information you voluntarily provide (which we assume means you're comfortable sharing it)

### Sensitive Personal Information

We do not process sensitive personal information.

### Information from Third Parties

We do not receive any information from third parties, ensuring your interactions with us remain straightforward and within our control.

### Authentication Data

We integrate with third-party authentication providers because building our own authentication system would require a level of responsibility we're not prepared to assume.

### Usage Data

We collect standard analytics data, which means whatever our analytics providers decide to track this week.

### Payment Information

We don't process sensitive information ourselves, but we do need to collect certain details to process any purchases you make. This includes:

- Payment Instrument Number
- Security Code Associated with Your Payment Instrument

Please note, all payment data is securely stored by our payment processors: LemonSqueezy, Stripe, and Polar. To understand how your payment information is handled, you can view Stripe’s privacy notice here.

## 3. How We Use Your Information

We process your personal information to enhance and manage our Services effectively, communicate with you, strengthen our security measures, prevent fraud, and comply with legal requirements. We assure you that your information is handled responsibly and only for the purposes of improving the services you receive from us. Depending on how you interact with our services, with your consent, we may also use your information for additional purposes:

- Create and manage your account (Account Management: We process your information for account setup, login, and maintenance to ensure a seamless user experience.)
- Provide and maintain our Services (Service Delivery: We use your information to fulfill service requests and ensure efficient delivery of our offerings.)
- Send important updates about our Services (Administrative Updates: We process your information to send you important updates about our products, services, and changes to our terms and policies.)
- Improve and optimize our Services (Trend Analysis: We analyze how you use our services to identify usage trends and make improvements.)
- Provide customer support (Support and Responses: Your information helps us address your questions and provide support for any service-related concerns.)
- Ensure security of our Services (Security Measures: We use your information to protect the integrity and security of our services, including monitoring for and preventing fraud.)
- Comply with legal obligations
- Feedback Requests: We may use your information to request feedback to enhance your experience and improve our services.

## 4. Information We Collect Automatically: Log Data.

Each time you visit {siteConfig.url}, our systems automatically gather information sent by your browser. This information, known as Log Data, includes:

- Internet Protocol (IP) Address of Your Computer
- Browser Version
- Pages of Our Service That You Visit
- Date and Time of Your Visit
- Time Spent on Those Pages
- Other Relevant Statistics

This data helps us understand how visitors use our website, which in turn allows us to improve our service and ensure a better user experience.

## 5. About Cookies.

Cookies are small files that serve as unique identifiers, which we send to your browser. They help us gather information to improve your experience on {siteConfig.url}. You have the option to either accept or decline these cookies. Please be aware that choosing to refuse cookies might restrict the functionality of certain aspects of our service.

## 6. Our Use of Service Providers.

We engage third-party companies and individuals to:

- Enhance our Service: They help us make our service better and more efficient for you.
- Perform Specific Tasks: These parties carry out service-related activities on our behalf.
- Analyze Service Usage: They assist us in understanding how our services are used by our community.

Please be aware that these third parties have access to your Personal Information. This access is solely for performing tasks on our behalf and they are strictly prohibited from using or disclosing your information for any other purpose.

## 7. How long do we store your information?

We hold onto your personal information only for as long as necessary, as outlined in our privacy notice. We will not retain your information for more than twelve (12) months following the termination of your account, except as required or permitted by law. Once we no longer have a legitimate business need for your data, we will either delete or anonymize it.

## 8. Information Sharing

We share information with:
- Authentication providers (GitHub, Discord, Google) to enable sign-in
- LemonSqueezy, Stripe, and Polar for payment processing
- Service providers who help us operate our Services
- Law enforcement when required by law

We do not sell your personal information to third parties.

## 9. Data Security

We value the trust you place in us by sharing your Personal Information. We strive to protect it using commercially reasonable methods. However, it’s important to understand that no method of transmitting information over the Internet or storing data electronically is completely secure. While we aim to use acceptable means to protect your information, absolute security cannot be guaranteed.

## 10. Links to External Websites.

Our service may provide links to external sites. When you click on a third-party link, you will be redirected to that site. These external sites are not operated by us, and we strongly advise you to review their Privacy Policy. Please note that we do not have control over the content, privacy policies, or practices of any third-party sites or services.

## 11. Your Rights

You can:
- Access your personal information
- Request corrections to your data
- Delete your account and associated data
- Export your data
- Choose which authentication providers to use

## 12. Future Updates to Our Services

We may introduce additional features that involve:
- Cookies and similar tracking technologies
- Additional analytics tools
- New authentication providers

We will update this policy and notify you before implementing such changes.

## 13. Children's Privacy

Our services are designed for individuals 13 years of age and older. We do not intentionally gather personally identifiable information from children under the age of 13. Should we discover that such information has been inadvertently collected on anyone under 13, we will take swift action to remove this information from our servers. If you are a parent or guardian and become aware that your child has provided us with personal information without your consent, please contact us so we can take appropriate measures.

## 14. International Users

Our Services are operated in the United States. If you are accessing our Services from outside the United States, please be aware that your information may be transferred to, stored, and processed in the United States.

For users in the European Economic Area (EEA), we comply with the GDPR. For California residents, we comply with the CCPA. This means you have certain rights regarding your personal data, including:

### For EEA Users (GDPR):
- Right to access
- Right to rectification
- Right to erasure ("right to be forgotten")
- Right to restrict processing
- Right to data portability
- Right to object to processing
- Rights related to automated decision-making

### For California Residents (CCPA):
- Right to know what personal information is collected
- Right to know whether personal information is sold or disclosed
- Right to say no to the sale of personal information
- Right to access personal information
- Right to equal service and price

## 15. Legal Basis for Processing.

We process your personal information only when necessary and with a valid legal basis under law, such as the General Data Protection Regulation (GDPR), which mandates us to clarify the legal grounds for our data processing activities.

## 16. Updates to Our Privacy Policy.

We may modify our Privacy Policy occasionally to reflect changes in our practices. We encourage you to visit this page periodically to stay informed about how we protect your personal information. Any updates will be posted on this page and are effective immediately upon posting.

## 17. Contact Us

If you have questions about this Privacy Policy or need to exercise your data protection rights, please feel free to contact us at:
- Email: {siteConfig.email.format("support")}

---

# Terms of Service

Last updated: 2025-01-01

## 1. Agreement to Terms

By accessing and using our software, services, and website (collectively, the "Services"), you agree to be bound by these Terms of Service ("Terms"). If you disagree with any part of these terms, you may not access the Services.

## 2. Description of Services

We provide a Next.js-based software development kit and related tools, including but not limited to starter templates, components, and documentation ("Platform"). Our Services are designed for developers and businesses building web applications.

## 3. User Accounts

### 3.1 Registration

To access certain features, you must register for an account. You agree to provide accurate, current, and complete information during registration and to update such information to keep it accurate, current, and complete.

### 3.2 Account Security

You are responsible for safeguarding your account credentials and for any activities or actions under your account. Notify us immediately of any unauthorized use of your account.

## 4. Intellectual Property Rights

### 4.1 Our Rights

The Platform, including its code, documentation, and content, is owned by us and is protected by copyright, trademark, and other intellectual property laws.

### 4.2 License Grant

We grant you a limited, non-exclusive, non-transferable license to use the Platform subject to these Terms and any associated license agreements.

### 4.3 User Content

You retain ownership of any content you create using our Platform. By using our Services, you grant us a worldwide, non-exclusive license to host and use your content to provide our Services.

## 5. Acceptable Use

You agree not to:

- Use the Services for any illegal purpose
- Distribute malware or other harmful code
- Attempt to gain unauthorized access to our systems
- Resell or redistribute the Platform without authorization
- Remove or modify any proprietary notices from the Platform

## 6. Payment Terms

### 6.1 Fees

Some features of our Services require payment. Fees are specified on our pricing page and may be updated from time to time.

### 6.2 Refunds

Refund policies are specified per subscription tier. Generally, we offer refunds within 30 days of purchase if you are unsatisfied with our Services.

## 7. Limitation of Liability

TO THE MAXIMUM EXTENT PERMITTED BY LAW, WE SHALL NOT BE LIABLE FOR ANY INDIRECT, INCIDENTAL, SPECIAL, CONSEQUENTIAL, OR PUNITIVE DAMAGES ARISING FROM YOUR USE OF THE SERVICES.

## 8. Termination

We may terminate or suspend your account and access to the Services at our sole discretion, without notice, for conduct that we believe violates these Terms or is harmful to other users, us, or third parties.

## 9. Changes to Terms

We reserve the right to modify these Terms at any time. We will notify users of any material changes via email or through the Platform.

## 10. Privacy Policy

Your use of the Services is also governed by our Privacy Policy, which is incorporated into these Terms by reference.

## 11. Contact Information

For questions about these Terms, please contact us at:

- Email: legal@shipkit.io

## 12. Governing Law

These Terms shall be governed by and construed in accordance with the laws of [Your Jurisdiction], without regard to its conflict of law provisions.

## 13. Severability

If any provision of these Terms is found to be unenforceable or invalid, that provision will be limited or eliminated to the minimum extent necessary so that these Terms will otherwise remain in full force and effect.

---

By using our Services, you acknowledge that you have read, understood, and agree to be bound by these Terms of Service.

# Legal Information

Last updated: 2025-07-15

All services on this website are provided by {siteConfig.creator.fullName}.

## Contact

Email: {siteConfig.email.format("support")}

## Liability and Copyright Notices
### Limitation of Liability
The content of this online offering has been created meticulously and according to our current state knowledge, but is for informational purposes only and does not create any legally binding effect, unless it concerns legally required information (e.g., imprint, privacy policy, terms of service, or mandatory consumer notices). We reserve the right to modify, partially or completely delete the content, provided the contractual obligations remain unaffected. All offers are non-binding.

### Links to Third-Party Websites
Contents from third-party websites that we directly or indirectly link to are beyond our area of responsibility and we do not endorse them. The provider of the linked websites is solely responsible for all content and particularly for damages resulting from the use of information accessible on these linked websites.

### Copyrights and Brand Rights
All content displayed on this website, such as text, photographs, graphics, brands, and trademarks are protected by the respective protective rights (copyright, brand rights). The use, reproduction, etc., are subject to our rights or the rights of the respective copyright holders or rights administrators.

### Notices of Legal Violations
If you discover any legal infringements within our web presence, please inform us. We will promptly remove any illegal content and links upon becoming aware of them.

# End User License Agreement (EULA) for Vercel Shipkit Integration

Last updated: 2024-07-01

This End User License Agreement ("EULA") is a legal agreement between you (either an individual or a single entity) and Shipkit ("we", "us", or "our") for the Vercel Shipkit Integration software ("Integration").

By installing, accessing, or using the Integration, you agree to be bound by the terms of this EULA. If you do not agree to the terms of this EULA, do not install or use the Integration.

## 1. Grant of License

Subject to the terms of this EULA, we grant you a limited, non-exclusive, non-transferable license to use the Integration solely for your personal or internal business purposes in conjunction with Vercel's services.

## 2. Description of Integration

The Vercel Shipkit Integration allows users to connect their Vercel accounts to Shipkit, enabling direct deployment of projects from Shipkit to Vercel. The Integration facilitates OAuth authentication between Shipkit and Vercel, and manages deployment workflows.

## 3. Vercel Account Requirements

### 3.1 Vercel Account

Use of the Integration requires a valid Vercel account. Your use of Vercel's services is governed by Vercel's own terms of service and privacy policy.

### 3.2 OAuth Authorization

The Integration uses OAuth 2.0 to authenticate with Vercel. By using the Integration, you authorize Shipkit to access your Vercel account with the permissions you approve during the OAuth flow.

## 4. Data Collection and Use

### 4.1 Account Information

The Integration collects and stores information from your Vercel account, including but not limited to user ID, access tokens, and project information, solely for the purpose of providing the Integration's functionality.

### 4.2 Deployment Data

Information about deployments initiated through the Integration may be collected and stored to provide service functionality and improvements.

## 5. Limitations

### 5.1 Usage Limits

The Integration may be subject to usage limits based on your Shipkit subscription tier. These limits may include the number of deployments, projects, or other metrics.

### 5.2 Modification Restrictions

You may not:
- Modify, reverse engineer, decompile, or disassemble the Integration
- Create derivative works based on the Integration
- Remove any proprietary notices or labels on the Integration
- Use the Integration in any way not expressly authorized by this EULA

## 6. Third-Party Services

### 6.1 Vercel Services

The Integration interacts with Vercel's API and services. We are not responsible for any issues, downtime, or changes in Vercel's services that may affect the Integration's functionality.

### 6.2 Other Integrations

If the Integration interacts with other third-party services, your use of those services is governed by their respective terms of service.

## 7. Updates and Maintenance

### 7.1 Updates

We may from time to time update the Integration to add features, fix bugs, or address security issues. These updates may be automatic or require your action.

### 7.2 Support

Support for the Integration is provided according to your Shipkit subscription tier. Support details are available on our website.

## 8. Termination

### 8.1 Termination by You

You may terminate this EULA by disconnecting the Integration from your Vercel account and ceasing all use of the Integration.

### 8.2 Termination by Us

We may terminate this EULA if you fail to comply with any of its terms. Upon termination, you must cease all use of the Integration.

## 9. Disclaimer of Warranties

THE INTEGRATION IS PROVIDED "AS IS" WITHOUT WARRANTY OF ANY KIND, EITHER EXPRESS OR IMPLIED, INCLUDING, BUT NOT LIMITED TO, THE IMPLIED WARRANTIES OF MERCHANTABILITY, FITNESS FOR A PARTICULAR PURPOSE, OR NON-INFRINGEMENT.

## 10. Limitation of Liability

TO THE MAXIMUM EXTENT PERMITTED BY APPLICABLE LAW, IN NO EVENT SHALL WE BE LIABLE FOR ANY SPECIAL, INCIDENTAL, INDIRECT, OR CONSEQUENTIAL DAMAGES WHATSOEVER (INCLUDING, WITHOUT LIMITATION, DAMAGES FOR LOSS OF BUSINESS PROFITS, BUSINESS INTERRUPTION, LOSS OF BUSINESS INFORMATION, OR ANY OTHER PECUNIARY LOSS) ARISING OUT OF THE USE OF OR INABILITY TO USE THE INTEGRATION.

## 11. Governing Law

This EULA shall be governed by and construed in accordance with the laws of the jurisdiction in which Shipkit operates, without regard to its conflict of law provisions.

## 12. Changes to this EULA

We reserve the right to modify this EULA at any time. We will notify users of any material changes via email or through the Shipkit platform.

## 13. Contact Information

If you have any questions about this EULA, please contact us at:

- Email: legal@shipkit.io
- Website: https://shipkit.io/contact

---

By using the Vercel Shipkit Integration, you acknowledge that you have read, understood, and agree to be bound by the terms of this EULA.

docs: Legal pages (privacy policy, terms of service, EULA, legal info) with MDX content and shared layout. Customize the content in each `.mdx` file after installation.
